sections.title: 03a — Constraint taxonomy coverage
sections.content:
| Type | # constraints | Absolute | Mitigable |
|---|---|---|---|
| physical | 4 | 2 | 2 |
| chemical | 2 | 2 | 0 |
| operational | 4 | 0 | 4 |
| economic | 3 | 0 | 3 |
| regulatory | 5 | 0 | 5 |
| environmental | 3 | 0 | 3 |
| **Total** | **21** | **4** | **17** |

All six types represented. Absolutes are physics/chemistry only (runaway chemistry, thermodynamic floor, electrolyte flammability, footprint floor). Every regulatory constraint has a certification path → mitigable per component rules.

---

sections.title: 03b — Enriched constraints (Step 07)
sections.content:
### Physical (4)

| # | Name | Threshold | Absolute | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | Land Footprint and Siting | 40–80 m²/MWh | false | Container footprint + safety clearances + Feuerwehr access; mitigable via multi-deck stacking, off-site lease, or project downsize. |
| P2 | Minimum EPC Project Scale | ≥500 kW / ≥1 MWh | false | Below this threshold, EPC overhead (grid cert, civil, project mgmt) exceeds equipment cost; mitigable by aggregating small sites into one contract. |
| P3 | Round-Trip Energy Loss Floor | 10–15 % | true | Thermodynamic floor: PCS switching + cell internal resistance + HVAC parasitic. Cannot go below ~10% AC-AC with current LFP+PCS stack. |
| P4 | Grid-Scale Power Density | 1 MWh per 20ft container | true | Volumetric limit of LFP (~160 Wh/L pack level); dictates site area per MWh — physics of cell chemistry. |

### Chemical (2)

| # | Name | Threshold | Absolute | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Onset | ~150–200 °C cell surface | true | Exothermic decomposition of cathode + electrolyte once onset reached. Cannot be eliminated — only contained via NOVEC/aerosol suppression, compartmentalization, and deflagration venting (VdS 3103). |
| C2 | Organic Electrolyte Flammability | flash-point ~30 °C (EMC/DMC) | true | Carbonate electrolytes are combustible; mandates fire-rated container, suppression, and gas-venting. Chemistry-inherent. |

### Operational (4)

| # | Name | Threshold | Absolute | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| O1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity (DSO) | site-specific kVA headroom | false | DSO must grant MV feeder capacity under VDE-AR-N 4110; where saturated, grid upgrade required (cost and schedule risk) or reduced project size. |
| O2 | Grid-Code Certification Lead Time | 6–12 months (TR8 Type B) | false | FGW TR8 plant cert and DSO acceptance on critical path. Parallelizable with procurement; partial mitigation via simplified Type B or early DSO engagement. |
| O3 | Minimum Duty Cycle for Business Case | ≥200 full-cycles/year | false | Below this throughput, revenue doesn't cover O&M + degradation amortization. Mitigable by stacking revenue streams (peak shaving + FCR + arbitrage). |
| O4 | SCADA/EMS Communication Link | IEC 61850 or IEC 60870-5-104 | false | DSO and aggregator dispatch require standards-compliant uplink; cellular or fiber dependency at remote sites. |

### Economic (3)

| # | Name | Threshold | Absolute | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| E1 | CAPEX Viability Ceiling | €250–400 /kWh installed | false | Above this, peak-shaving/arbitrage business case closes at >7-yr payback. Mitigable by revenue stacking or subsidy (KfW 293/270, innovation tenders). |
| E2 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | >80% LFP cells from CN | false | FX, tariff, CRMA, and EU Battery Regulation compliance risk. Mitigable via multi-vendor qualification and EU-origin cell premium. |
| E3 | Minimum Revenue-Stream Access | ≥1 active monetization path | false | Peak shaving, §19 StromNEV, EPEX arbitrage, FCR/aFRR prequal. Without revenue pathway the asset is stranded. Mitigable via aggregator contract. |

### Regulatory (5)

| # | Name | Threshold | Absolute | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| R1 | VDE-AR-N 4110 MV Grid Code | mandatory for ≥1 kV or >100 kW | false | Certification path is standard Mein Solar scope. Blocker only where DSO refuses feeder. |
| R2 | FGW TR8 Plant Certification | Type B plant cert mandatory | false | Accredited certifier (TÜV, DEKRA) required. Certification path exists and proven. |
| R3 | Fire-Protection Clearance (VdS 3103 / Feuerwehr) | separation walls for >3 MWh | false | Local Feuerwehr acceptance needed; clearances dictate siting but always engineerable. |
| R4 | Baugenehmigung (Municipal Building Permit) | varies by Bundesland | false | Container = building under most LBO. 3–9 month permit cycle; commune discretion is the risk, not the rule. |
| R5 | EU Battery Regulation + CRMA | Passport + due diligence mandatory from 2027 | false | Carbon footprint declaration, supply-chain due diligence, recycling chain required. Compliance effort, not a legal barrier. |

### Environmental (3)

| # | Name | Threshold | Absolute | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V1 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | -20 to +50 °C | false | HVAC holds internal setpoint; extremes reduce RTE and cycle life. Mitigable via upgraded cooling or heated enclosure. |
| V2 | Altitude Derating | >1000 m ASL | false | PCS and MV transformer require derating factor above 1000 m; standard OEM option but reduces effective MW. |
| V3 | Seismic / Flood Site Risk | DIN 4149 zone, HQ100 floodplain | false | Foundation design and elevated plinth mitigate. Restricts siting, doesn't prevent it. |

---

sections.title: 03c — Affected-market hints (constraint → candidate NAICS domains)
sections.content:
Maps each constraint to 2–5 industry/domain hints for Step 08. Step 02 market list will be filtered against this.

| # | Constraint | Affected market hints |
|---|---|---|
| P1 | Land Footprint | Urban industrial campuses, data centers, airports, dense retail, rooftop-only C&I |
| P2 | Minimum EPC Scale | Small commercial (<500 kW), single-family residential, agri-PV micro-sites, SME retail |
| P3 | RTE Floor | High-frequency arbitrage, short-duration FCR, thin-margin utility dispatch |
| P4 | Power Density | Underground/vaulted substations, confined brownfield sites |
| C1 | Thermal Runaway Onset | Hospitals, schools, data centers, high-occupancy buildings, co-located with H2 |
| C2 | Electrolyte Flammability | Petrochemical sites (ATEX zones), fuel terminals, co-located flammable-storage |
| O1 | MV Grid Capacity | Rural DE (Brandenburg, MV, Niedersachsen), saturated urban feeders, weak-grid islands |
| O2 | Certification Lead Time | Time-critical projects (<6 months), emergency backup deployments |
| O3 | Duty Cycle | Pure-backup UPS substitution, extreme-seasonal-only loads |
| O4 | SCADA Link | Remote off-grid sites, sites without fiber/cellular coverage |
| E1 | CAPEX Ceiling | Markets with <€0.10/kWh price spread, flat tariff structures, regulated monopoly grids |
| E2 | Supply Chain | EU-content-mandated public procurement (defense, critical infra) |
| E3 | Revenue Streams | Monopoly utility territories with no arbitrage/ancillary market |
| R1 | VDE-AR-N 4110 | Non-DE markets (AT uses TOR-E; CH uses NA/EEA; FR uses Enedis code) |
| R2 | FGW TR8 | Non-DE; outside DACH |
| R3 | Fire Clearance | Dense urban, co-located with residential, tunnel/underground sites |
| R4 | Baugenehmigung | Conservation zones (Denkmalschutz), Natura-2000 sites, protected landscapes |
| R5 | EU Battery Reg | EU-external deployments (UK post-Brexit, CH, non-EU exports) |
| V1 | Ambient Temp | Desert solar (Middle East/N. Africa), Arctic/sub-Arctic, outdoor marine |
| V2 | Altitude | Alpine hydro sites, high-altitude mining, Andean/Himalayan grids |
| V3 | Seismic/Flood | Coastal flood zones, DIN 4149 zones 2–3, fault-line territories |

---

sections.title: 03d — Knockout-prone patterns (pre-Step-08 guidance)
sections.content:
Flags which constraint-market pairs would likely be genuine **knockouts** vs. **mitigable** once Step 02 markets arrive. Applied as decision rules in Step 08a.

| Pattern | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Non-DE / non-DACH market × R1–R2 | **mitigable** | Different grid code regime required (AT TOR, CH NA/EEA, FR Enedis). Engineering effort but not impossible. |
| Non-EU export market × R5 | **none** | EU Battery Regulation does not bind outside EU. |
| Site <500 kW peak load × P2 | **knockout** | Below minimum EPC project scale — business economics vanish below 1 MWh regardless of revenue. *Exception*: aggregated multi-site portfolios → mitigable. |
| Market with routine >50 °C ambient × V1 | **mitigable** | Extended-temp HVAC packages exist (Middle East BESS deployments prove feasibility). |
| Market with routine <-30 °C ambient × V1 | **mitigable** | Heated enclosure + battery pre-conditioning (Nordic projects prove feasibility). |
| Hydrogen-refueling co-location × C2 | **mitigable** | Requires additional ATEX zoning and separation; engineering solvable. |
| ATEX Zone 0/1 co-located installation × C2 | **knockout** | Lithium+organic electrolyte inherently non-ATEX; separation distance is the only answer, and if site topology forbids separation → genuine physics block. |
| Market requiring <3-month delivery × O2 | **knockout** | TR8 Type B cert cannot compress below ~6 months; cert is prerequisite to energization. |
| Market with zero revenue stream access × E3 | **knockout** | No monetization path = no business case. Mitigable only if regulatory reform is on a known timeline. |
| Denkmalschutz / UNESCO heritage site × R4 | **knockout** | Containerized industrial asset incompatible with heritage protection. No mitigation path. |
| Market with no DSO feeder capacity AND no upgrade path × O1 | **knockout** | If DSO formally refuses connection AND no alternative POI exists, project is infeasible. |
| All other regulatory constraints × any market | **mitigable** | Every cert (ATEX adjacency, CE, VdS, building permit) has an engineering path. |

**Cost/performance are never knockouts** — CAPEX exceedance is mitigable (E1), RTE floor is a none/mitigable (P3). Applied per component doc false-positive rules.

---

sections.title: 03e — Extension-axis constraint overrides
sections.content:
Constraints differ across the FP-extension axes from Step 02e. Same EPC methodology, different asset → different physics.

| Extension domain | Constraints that drop | Constraints that change |
|---|---|---|
| Flow-battery EPC (vanadium, iron-salt) | C1 (runaway), C2 (flammability) — aqueous electrolyte non-flammable | P4 density relaxes (lower kWh/m²); R5 unchanged |
| Flywheel / CAES EPC | C1, C2, E2 (no cells), R5 (no battery) | P1 footprint per MWh much larger; P3 RTE floor higher (~30–40%) |
| Thermal-to-power storage EPC | C1, C2, R5 | Adds thermal-insulation and heat-source constraints |
| HPC charging hub EPC (no BESS) | C1, C2, R5, E2 | R1, R2, O1 still apply; P1 footprint per dispenser instead of per MWh |
| Large-scale PV EPC | C1, C2, R5 | R1, R2, O1, R4 apply; P1 footprint dominated by modules (10–15 MW/ha) |
| Industrial microgrid EPC | — | Adds R-islanding, black-start, and protection-coordination constraints |

**Implication:** Markets knocked out by BESS-specific constraints (C1, C2, R5) may still be addressable via the extension axes. Step 08 must run per-product-scope; Mein Solar's broader EPC capability is a second-pass pipeline.

---

sections.title: 03f — Validation notes (Step 07c equivalent)
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| All 6 constraint types represented | ✅ (4/2/4/3/5/3) |
| is_absolute=true limited to physics/chemistry | ✅ (P3, P4, C1, C2 only) |
| No regulation classified as absolute | ✅ (all R* have cert path) |
| Thresholds use numeric values + units | ✅ (all quantified thresholds numeric; qualitative constraints carry explicit threshold narrative) |
| Environmental vs. chemical separated | ✅ (V1–V3 = ambient; C1–C2 = cells/electrolyte) |
| Cost never treated as knockout | ✅ (E1 is mitigable) |
| Knockout-prone patterns flagged | ✅ (§03d) |
| Extension axes cross-referenced | ✅ (§03e) |

---

sections.title: 03g — Neo4j write preview
sections.content:
```
(NeutralProductGroup {name:"Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC"})
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint {constraint_type:"physical"})   x4
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint {constraint_type:"chemical"})   x2
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint {constraint_type:"operational"})x4
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint {constraint_type:"economic"})   x3
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint {constraint_type:"regulatory"}) x5
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint {constraint_type:"environmental"}) x3

// Step 08 deferred — requires MarketDiscoveryResult from Step 02.
// (Constraint)-[:incompatible_with {compatibility,rationale,...}]->(Market)
// Market.status = "eliminated_by_constraint"  for knocked-out markets
```

---


rows.Name: Land Footprint and Siting
rows.Description: Container footprint + safety clearances + Feuerwehr access; mitigable via multi-deck stacking, off-site lease, or project downsize.

rows.Name: Minimum EPC Project Scale
rows.Description: Below this threshold, EPC overhead (grid cert, civil, project mgmt) exceeds equipment cost; mitigable by aggregating small sites into one contract.

rows.Name: Round-Trip Energy Loss Floor
rows.Description: Thermodynamic floor: PCS switching + cell internal resistance + HVAC parasitic. Cannot go below ~10% AC-AC with current LFP+PCS stack.

rows.Name: Grid-Scale Power Density
rows.Description: Volumetric limit of LFP (~160 Wh/L pack level); dictates site area per MWh — physics of cell chemistry.

rows.Name: Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Onset
rows.Description: Exothermic decomposition of cathode + electrolyte once onset reached. Cannot be eliminated — only contained via NOVEC/aerosol suppression, compartmentalization, and deflagration venting (VdS 3103).

rows.Name: Organic Electrolyte Flammability
rows.Description: Carbonate electrolytes are combustible; mandates fire-rated container, suppression, and gas-venting. Chemistry-inherent.

rows.Name: MV Grid Connection Capacity (DSO)
rows.Description: DSO must grant MV feeder capacity under VDE-AR-N 4110; where saturated, grid upgrade required (cost and schedule risk) or reduced project size.

rows.Name: Grid-Code Certification Lead Time
rows.Description: FGW TR8 plant cert and DSO acceptance on critical path. Parallelizable with procurement; partial mitigation via simplified Type B or early DSO engagement.

rows.Name: Minimum Duty Cycle for Business Case
rows.Description: Below this throughput, revenue doesn't cover O&M + degradation amortization. Mitigable by stacking revenue streams (peak shaving + FCR + arbitrage).

rows.Name: SCADA/EMS Communication Link
rows.Description: DSO and aggregator dispatch require standards-compliant uplink; cellular or fiber dependency at remote sites.

rows.Name: CAPEX Viability Ceiling
rows.Description: Above this, peak-shaving/arbitrage business case closes at >7-yr payback. Mitigable by revenue stacking or subsidy (KfW 293/270, innovation tenders).

rows.Name: Battery Supply Chain Concentration
rows.Description: FX, tariff, CRMA, and EU Battery Regulation compliance risk. Mitigable via multi-vendor qualification and EU-origin cell premium.

rows.Name: Minimum Revenue-Stream Access
rows.Description: Peak shaving, §19 StromNEV, EPEX arbitrage, FCR/aFRR prequal. Without revenue pathway the asset is stranded. Mitigable via aggregator contract.

rows.Name: VDE-AR-N 4110 MV Grid Code
rows.Description: Certification path is standard Mein Solar scope. Blocker only where DSO refuses feeder.

rows.Name: FGW TR8 Plant Certification
rows.Description: Accredited certifier (TÜV, DEKRA) required. Certification path exists and proven.

rows.Name: Fire-Protection Clearance (VdS 3103 / Feuerwehr)
rows.Description: Local Feuerwehr acceptance needed; clearances dictate siting but always engineerable.

rows.Name: Baugenehmigung (Municipal Building Permit)
rows.Description: Container = building under most LBO. 3–9 month permit cycle; commune discretion is the risk, not the rule.

rows.Name: EU Battery Regulation + CRMA
rows.Description: Carbon footprint declaration, supply-chain due diligence, recycling chain required. Compliance effort, not a legal barrier.

rows.Name: Ambient Temperature Envelope
rows.Description: HVAC holds internal setpoint; extremes reduce RTE and cycle life. Mitigable via upgraded cooling or heated enclosure.

rows.Name: Altitude Derating
rows.Description: PCS and MV transformer require derating factor above 1000 m; standard OEM option but reduces effective MW.

rows.Name: Seismic / Flood Site Risk
rows.Description: Foundation design and elevated plinth mitigate. Restricts siting, doesn't prevent it.

rows.Affected market hints: Urban industrial campuses, data centers, airports, dense retail, rooftop-only C&I
Small commercial (<500 kW), single-family residential, agri-PV micro-sites, SME retail
High-frequency arbitrage, short-duration FCR, thin-margin utility dispatch
Underground/vaulted substations, confined brownfield sites
Hospitals, schools, data centers, high-occupancy buildings, co-located with H2
Petrochemical sites (ATEX zones), fuel terminals, co-located flammable-storage
Rural DE (Brandenburg, MV, Niedersachsen), saturated urban feeders, weak-grid islands
Time-critical projects (<6 months), emergency backup deployments
Pure-backup UPS substitution, extreme-seasonal-only loads
Remote off-grid sites, sites without fiber/cellular coverage
Markets with <€0.10/kWh price spread, flat tariff structures, regulated monopoly grids
EU-content-mandated public procurement (defense, critical infra)
Monopoly utility territories with no arbitrage/ancillary market
Non-DE markets (AT uses TOR-E; CH uses NA/EEA; FR uses Enedis code)
Non-DE; outside DACH
Dense urban, co-located with residential, tunnel/underground sites
Conservation zones (Denkmalschutz), Natura-2000 sites, protected landscapes
EU-external deployments (UK post-Brexit, CH, non-EU exports)
Desert solar (Middle East/N. Africa), Arctic/sub-Arctic, outdoor marine
Alpine hydro sites, high-altitude mining, Andean/Himalayan grids
Coastal flood zones, DIN 4149 zones 2–3, fault-line territories

rows.Rationale: Different grid code regime required (AT TOR, CH NA/EEA, FR Enedis). Engineering effort but not impossible.
EU Battery Regulation does not bind outside EU.
Below minimum EPC project scale — business economics vanish below 1 MWh regardless of revenue. *Exception*: aggregated multi-site portfolios → mitigable.
Extended-temp HVAC packages exist (Middle East BESS deployments prove feasibility).
Heated enclosure + battery pre-conditioning (Nordic projects prove feasibility).
Requires additional ATEX zoning and separation; engineering solvable.
Lithium+organic electrolyte inherently non-ATEX; separation distance is the only answer, and if site topology forbids separation → genuine physics block.
TR8 Type B cert cannot compress below ~6 months; cert is prerequisite to energization.

rows.Pattern: Market with zero revenue stream access × E3
rows.Rationale: No monetization path = no business case. Mitigable only if regulatory reform is on a known timeline.
Containerized industrial asset incompatible with heritage protection. No mitigation path.

rows.Pattern: Market with no DSO feeder capacity AND no upgrade path × O1
rows.Rationale: If DSO formally refuses connection AND no alternative POI exists, project is infeasible.

rows.Pattern: All other regulatory constraints × any market
rows.Rationale: Every cert (ATEX adjacency, CE, VdS, building permit) has an engineering path.

rows.Constraints that change: P4 density relaxes (lower kWh/m²); R5 unchanged

rows.Constraints that drop: C1, C2, E2 (no cells), R5 (no battery)
rows.Constraints that change: P1 footprint per MWh much larger; P3 RTE floor higher (~30–40%)
R1, R2, O1 still apply; P1 footprint per dispenser instead of per MWh
R1, R2, O1, R4 apply; P1 footprint dominated by modules (10–15 MW/ha)
Adds R-islanding, black-start, and protection-coordination constraints

rows.Status: ✅ (all quantified thresholds numeric; qualitative constraints carry explicit threshold narrative)

entities.technology_class: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC

constraints.name: Land Footprint and Siting
constraints.description: Container footprint plus safety clearances and Feuerwehr access driven by site layout; mitigable via stacked decks, off-site lease, or project resizing.

constraints.name: Minimum EPC Project Scale
constraints.description: Below ~1 MWh the EPC overhead (cert, civil, PM) exceeds equipment cost; project-delivery model breaks even only at grid-scale.

constraints.name: Round-Trip Energy Loss Floor
constraints.description: Thermodynamic floor from PCS switching, cell internal resistance, and HVAC parasitic. Cannot drop below ~10% AC-AC with current LFP+PCS stack.

constraints.name: Grid-Scale Power Density Limit
constraints.description: LFP pack-level energy density ~160 Wh/L; 1 MWh per 20ft container is the practical limit for containerized stationary storage.

constraints.name: Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Onset
constraints.description: Exothermic cathode+electrolyte decomposition once onset reached. Cannot be eliminated, only contained via NOVEC/aerosol, compartmentalization, and deflagration venting (VdS 3103).

constraints.name: Organic Electrolyte Flammability
constraints.description: Carbonate electrolytes (EMC/DMC/EC) are combustible with flash points near ambient; mandates fire-rated envelope and suppression. Chemistry-inherent.

constraints.name: MV Grid Connection Capacity (DSO)
constraints.description: DSO must grant MV feeder capacity under VDE-AR-N 4110; saturated grids force upgrade or downsizing; in rare cases DSO refuses connection outright.

constraints.name: Grid-Code Certification Lead Time
constraints.description: FGW TR8 Type B plant certificate and DSO acceptance lengthen schedule; parallelizable with procurement but always on the critical path.

constraints.name: Minimum Duty Cycle for Business Case
constraints.description: Below ~200 full-cycles/yr revenue does not cover O&M + degradation amortization. Mitigable by stacking revenue streams.

constraints.name: SCADA/EMS Communication Link
constraints.description: DSO and aggregator dispatch require standards-compliant IEC 61850 or IEC 60870-5-104 uplink; cellular or fiber dependency at remote sites.

constraints.name: CAPEX Viability Ceiling
constraints.description: Above €400/kWh installed the peak-shaving/arbitrage case closes at >7-yr payback. Mitigable via revenue stacking or KfW/innovation subsidies.

constraints.name: Battery Supply Chain Concentration
constraints.description: LFP cells concentrated in CN suppliers; FX, tariff, CRMA and EU Battery Reg compliance risk. Mitigable via multi-vendor qualification.

constraints.name: Minimum Revenue-Stream Access
constraints.description: Need at least one monetization path (peak shaving, §19 StromNEV, EPEX arbitrage, FCR/aFRR prequal). No revenue = stranded asset.

constraints.name: VDE-AR-N 4110 MV Grid Code Compliance
constraints.description: Mandatory for connections ≥1 kV or >100 kW in Germany. Certification path is standard Mein Solar scope.
affected_markets_hint: Non-DE markets (AT TOR-E, CH NA/EEA, FR Enedis)

constraints.name: FGW TR8 Plant Certification
constraints.description: Type B simplified plant certificate mandatory for MV-connected storage plants; accredited certifier (TÜV, DEKRA) required.

constraints.name: Fire-Protection Clearance (VdS 3103 / Feuerwehr)
constraints.description: Minimum distance to buildings/property line, hydrant access, separation walls for >3 MWh clusters per VdS 3103 and local Feuerwehr guidance.

constraints.name: Baugenehmigung (Municipal Building Permit)
constraints.description: Container = building under most Landesbauordnung. 3–9 month permit cycle; municipal discretion is the schedule risk.

constraints.name: EU Battery Regulation + CRMA Compliance
constraints.description: Digital battery passport, carbon footprint declaration, supply-chain due diligence, and recycling chain mandatory from 2027. Compliance effort, not a legal barrier.

constraints.name: Ambient Temperature Envelope
constraints.description: HVAC maintains internal setpoint; extremes reduce RTE and cycle life. Mitigable via upgraded cooling or heated enclosure packages.

constraints.name: Altitude Derating
constraints.description: PCS and MV transformer require derating above 1000 m ASL (standard OEM option) — reduces effective MW rating.

constraints.name: Seismic / Flood Site Risk
constraints.description: Foundation design (DIN 4149 zones) and elevated plinth (HQ100 floodplain) mitigate; restricts siting, doesn't prevent it.

knockout_prone_patterns.rationale: Below EPC economic floor; no business case irrespective of revenue stacking.

knockout_prone_patterns.pattern: ATEX Zone 0/1 co-located, no separation distance available
knockout_prone_patterns.rationale: Lithium electrolyte chemistry is inherently non-ATEX; if site topology forbids separation distance, no engineering path exists.
FGW TR8 Type B cert floor is ~6 months and cannot be compressed; cert is mandatory prerequisite to grid connection.

knockout_prone_patterns.pattern: Zero revenue-stream access (no arbitrage, no ancillary, no peak-shaving tariff)
knockout_prone_patterns.rationale: Without monetization the business case fails; mitigable only if regulatory reform is on a known timeline.
Containerized industrial asset incompatible with heritage-protection regime.

knockout_prone_patterns.pattern: DSO formally refuses connection AND no alternative POI
knockout_prone_patterns.rationale: Without a grid connection point the asset cannot operate; grid upgrade must be either possible or cost-tolerable.
Different grid-code regime (AT, CH, FR, etc.) requires engineering effort but is not impossible; certification path exists everywhere.
Extended-temp HVAC or heated enclosure packages are standard OEM options; MENA and Nordic BESS deployments prove feasibility.
Cost is never a knockout; mitigable via subsidies, revenue stacking, or project resizing.

constraints_modified: P1 footprint per dispenser instead of per MWh
P1 footprint ~10-15 MW/ha dominated by modules
